meaning: To be extremely busy with too much work
example: I can't go to lunch, I'm swamped with deadlines

spanish: llamar para decir que estás enfermo
meaning: To notify your employer that you won't come to work due to illness
example: I'm not feeling well, I think I'll call in sick tomorrow

topic: to get fired / to get laid off
spanish: ser despedido / ser despedido por recortes
meaning: Fired = dismissed for cause; Laid off = dismissed due to cuts
example: He got fired for being late. She got laid off when the company downsized

meaning: The latest time by which something must be completed
example: The deadline for the project is next Friday

meaning: To work beyond your normal hours
example: I've been working overtime every day this week

spanish: hacer varias cosas a la vez
meaning: To do multiple tasks at the same time
example: I'm great at multitasking — I can answer emails while on a call

meaning: To generate ideas together as a group
example: Let's brainstorm some solutions in tomorrow's meeting

meaning: To check on the progress of something after initial contact
example: I'll follow up with the client next week about the proposal

topic: to be promoted / to get a raise
meaning: Promoted = higher position; Raise = higher salary
example: She got promoted to manager and got a 20% raise

meaning: Working from a location outside the office, usually home
example: I work from home three days a week now

meaning: To build professional relationships and connections
example: I met some great people while networking at the conference

meaning: To voluntarily leave your job
example: He resigned after finding a better opportunity

topic: to be on the clock / off the clock
spanish: estar en horario laboral / fuera de horario
meaning: On the clock = during work hours; Off the clock = outside work hours
example: I don't check emails when I'm off the clock

meaning: To include someone as a recipient in an email
example: Please cc my manager on that email

meaning: To not work on a day you normally would
example: I'm taking Friday off to go to the dentist

meaning: A short daily team meeting, often standing up
example: We have a 15-minute standup every morning at 9

meaning: To become exhausted from too much work or stress
example: I burned out after working 60-hour weeks for months

meaning: To integrate a new employee into the company
example: HR will onboard you during your first week

meaning: To briefly check in or reconnect with someone
example: Let's touch base next week about the project status

meaning: A secondary job or business in addition to your main job
example: My side hustle is selling handmade jewelry online
